title: Connection lost due to Nginx WebSocket handling
symptom: Users experience a 'Connection lost' message in the workflow editor when using Nginx as a reverse proxy. This occurs after successfully logging into n8n.
applies_when: Using Nginx as a reverse proxy
not_this_if: Nginx is not used as a reverse proxy
root_cause: Nginx does not handle WebSocket connections by default, which is necessary for real-time updates in the n8n workflow editor. Without proper configuration, the connection will drop, leading to the 'Connection lost' message.
fix_steps: Add WebSocket support to your Nginx configuration by including the following lines: proxy_http_version 1.1; proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade; proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';

Ensure that the following headers are set: proxy_set_header Host $host; proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
Disable caching and buffering by setting: chunked_transfer_encoding off; proxy_buffering off; proxy_cache off;
Increase timeout settings with: proxy_read_timeout 3600; proxy_send_timeout 3600;
Reload the Nginx configuration to apply the changes.
agent_instruction: Update the Nginx configuration to handle WebSocket connections and reload Nginx.
